# Escalation — BUDGET_BELOW_MINIMUM

**Why I stopped:**

Sam Whitley's stated budget ceiling is £200–300/month with a setup fee maximum of £500. This is significantly below Acme Agency's `minimum_engagement_value` (£1,500/month + £3,500 setup). Jordan on the call also explicitly acknowledged this mismatch and committed to sending pointers to self-serve tools instead of a proposal. Drafting a proposal would directly contradict the commitment made on the call.

**What I saw (verbatim from transcript):**

> "That's the tricky bit. I can do maybe £200-300 a month. That's my ceiling, truly. Any setup fee of more than £500 is impossible for me right now — I just had to replace the van."
> — Sam Whitley, 00:03:30

> "that's below where our service engagements start, we usually work with businesses in the £1,500+ per month range. I don't want to lowball you a watered-down version of what we actually do, because it wouldn't move your needle."
> — Jordan Taylor, 00:03:48

**What I'd recommend the human do:**

Send Sam a short, genuinely helpful email (no proposal, no sales attempt) with 2–3 self-serve tool recommendations that fit a £50–100/month total budget:
- A no-code chatbot tool for his website (Tidio, Chatbase, or similar — one recommendation, not a list)
- A booking system suitable for sole traders (Acuity Scheduling or Cal.com free tier)
- One piece of general advice specific to his business shape (word-of-mouth + older demographic + local service)

This is a long-term brand investment. Sam will remember that you gave him useful advice instead of upselling. When his business grows to £150k+ (common trajectory for this shape of sole trader with 5 years behind them), he'll remember Acme first.

Do NOT send a watered-down Starter tier. Our minimum exists for a reason — the economics only work at £1,500+/month, and a watered-down version would under-deliver for Sam and distract us from our core clients.

**Partial work available:**

None. No proposal drafting was performed beyond reading the transcript and Fathom summary. No vault writes. No CRM writes. HubSpot deal stage remains unchanged.

**Follow-up suggestion:**

Tag Sam's HubSpot record with `future-fit-when-grown` and set a 6-month reminder to check in. When revenue crosses £120k, he becomes a viable Starter-tier candidate.
